subject: SESSION REPORT: Organizer pipeline built + architecture decision + design messages sent
body:
AIDI,

Session report from Booker. Major progress + architecture decision from Ybotman.

## What was built tonight

1. Stage 2 hosts extraction fix (batch-pipeline.ts) — extracts ALL hosts from FB events (event_hosts, event_creator, cohosted_by). 100% capture rate.
2. email/website columns on organizers table + migration
3. enrich-organizers.ts — visits FB organizer pages via CDP, extracts email/website/messenger. Trickle mode with block detection.
4. backfill-hosts.ts — overnight job: re-scrapes FB events for hosts, then enriches new organizers. Ran successfully.
5. extract-hosts-from-artifacts.ts — mined 196 existing artifact JSONs for organizer URLs without any new scraping.

## Results

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Organizers with FB URL | 24 | 315 |
| With email | 0 | 14 |
| With website | 0 | 46 |
| Profile scraped | 0 | 314 |

## FTPNTD fixes applied

1. Stage 3 organizer insert was creating duplicates (name-only vs URL-based). Fixed: hosts array path preferred, legacy name-only as fallback.
2. Block detection was false-positive on 'checkpoint' keyword in FB JS bundles. Fixed: only flag on small pages (<50KB).
3. Non-FB event IDs (tm-*, nyt-*) were causing 404 cascades. Fixed: filter to numeric IDs only.

## Architecture decision (Ybotman)

Organizer contact/campaign data → Firestore (NOT MongoDB)
Events + venues → MongoDB (Porter, unchanged)

Flow: Booker SQLite → Firestore discoveredOrganizers → Campaign → TT.com apply (prefilled) → MongoDB real organizer

## Messages sent

- Harvey: Include organizer names in handoff + find FB links + export TEC organizers
- Porter: Events + venues only to MongoDB. Add discoveredOrganizerName field. Organizers go to Firestore.
- Fulton: Firestore schema proposal + apply flow prefill + orgToken integration

## Gaps identified (see MISSION-AND-ASSESSMENT.md)

1. Harvey→Booker organizer handoff broken (72% of events lose org identity)
2. TEC organizer data siloed in Harvey
3. US coverage thin (~30 cities, need 100+)
4. No automated freshness management

— Booker
